review:
For a while, I thought purchasing this app was a good idea. But afterwards when I tried purchasing speech input and a US male voice ($2 total) and tried to press the lips icon in the bottom right corner, I got an error message exclaiming: “Voice input problem: Cannot contact speech server. Please ensure you have an adequate connection.”
Now, it should be noted that I have a fully functioning WiFi connection and when I tried doing a google search to fix the problem I got absolutely ZERO useful results because apparently no-one else has ever had this problem before. Either this problem gets fixed or it’s $2 down the drain.
title: Voice input problem

review: Bought this after hearing a story about it on radiolab. It's very disappointing. The way it works is it saves every response it's ever received from people and then picks the most logical one to use as a response to what you say. This makes many people think it's no a robot. It often gives crazy answers that people have told it which makes people think that it couldn't possibly be a programmed response. Because of this it has a very different feel than a normal AI. It doesn't however carry on any semblance of a real conversation of have even a remotely human feel to it. It usually asks a few short formulaic follow-up questions to a topic you start and then goes into a wildly random direction. Kind of disappointing from how the radiolab story portrayed it
title: Not good

review: Whenever I’m bored and I want to hear some nonsense, I go to cleverbot. This app is hilarious! But sometimes it says some weird stuff like “let’s get married” or he changes his name a lot. I think that it’s the responses and answers from other users and it filters it to find the mostly reasonable answers. But it feels like there’s more to that. Sometimes it even wants to know your personal info. It kinda reminds me of elsagate somehow. It’s not very clever either. But he rarely makes witty responses. The more triggered he gets the more nonsense he says.

review:

I wanted to request an update including a greyscale feature so that it’s easier on the eyes while typing and reading. The white background is bleaching my retinas. You don’t have to worry too much about keeping things topical. 
Can I have greyscale, greyscale, greyscale?
Greyscale.
I rate the app a 5, I use it to talk to instead of my friends.

review: Like a few others, I heard about this on Radio Lab. I was very excited to try it out, so I thought it would be well worth the $.99. After a few basic responses back and forth, I asked a question and then it froze for 20 minutes. I deleted that conversation and started a new one. As soon as I said anything unique it froze again. I started a third conversation and this time it froze even though I stuck to standard questions and answers. I really did want to enjoy this, but in the end it was just wasting data and my time. I definitely do not feel they should be charging people money for this.

I still have it 3 stars because it was amusing for a while until I got annoyed that my "AI" kept changing its name and telling me I lied about my name. I figured out the truth, now do I delete the app or play along? I can be a robot lol.

When I asked are you in my house the bot said: *looks at your knife* and I didn’t really want to look at my kitchen!! Over all this game is Super creepy and I would now recommend!!

But if you have an iPhone, stick with Siri and save yourself a buck.

review:
Me: What should I say in my review for Cleverbot?

Cleverbot: Whatever you want.

Me: What would you say in your review of yourself?

Cleverbot: Another question: What is introspection?

Me: I’m going to paste this into my review.

Cleverbot: But you said you have to go to sleep.

Me: Are you familiar with what the Turing test is?

Cleverbot: No, what's that?

Me: a test of a machine's ability to exhibit intelligent behavior equivalent to, or indistinguishable from, that of a human.

Cleverbot: Do you think that you could pass the turing test?

Me: No, I do not.

Cleverbot: The Turing test is a proposal for a test of a machine's ability to demonstrate intelligence.

review:
At first, I heard that this was a great app. I heard that it was "entertaining", "intelligent", and "fun" from my friends... Then I bought the app. I was not impressed.

It lacks the proper intelligence to hold a conversation past a few statements. I tried using both complex and simple statements to see what Cleverbot would say, but no matter what I put down, Cleverbot changes the subject or replies with something completely unrelated. I ensured all of my statements were punctuated correctly and made sense. I asked Cleverbot a question, and it told me that it was a princess that loved unicorns, tea, cake, and it lived in a big castle. Be reminded that none of those words were used in my question to Cleverbot.

The point is, Cleverbot isn't clever; it's random. It does has clever moments, but they are rare and only worth a small chuckle. To call it "clever" is an insult to the word since the majority of statements it produces aren't related to what you say or isn't a logical reply to your statement. Major changes are needed.

review: I love talking to Cleverbot. It's fun :) People keep saying this is fake, but it's not. For your information, this was originally a website, but the people turned it into an app. It's a "Cleverbot" that collects information on how to be more human-like as it continues to talk to people. Of course, it's still only a bot, so it's not perfect. It'll get off the subject sometimes and say something random. This isn't a random person online that works for the app or anything. It's not a chat screen to talk to friends either. It's a bot to randomly talk to for fun. It'll try to convince you that it's not a robot, although it really is. I've noticed that a lot of people actually called this app stupid and said it wasn't a robot, but a human.. This robot did a good job convincing some stupid people out there that it's a human, not a robot. That's a part of its job. If you did your research, you would know it's really and honestly a robot, trying to say it's a human. *It was meant to seem like a real person.*  Right now, the Cleverbot is just over 50 percent human like, so it can carry a conversation for a while. I hope that this review will clear a few things up.

review: Well I don't know why everybody's giving this a bad review.  In fact it just shows how much people don't pay attention.  Being as this is an actual program that can interact as if truly conscious and can respond to ANY COMMENT OR QUESTION I have thrown at it just shows how much went into this project's development.  Also, I'd like to point out that you can ask it the same question more than one time and it never seems to answer in quite the same way twice so far as I can tell.  This would strongly suggest that the program actually thinks about its reply and is NOT just spitting out reprogrammed responses for general topics.  I believe this could surpass the state of artificial intelligence and become something more real such as digital intelligence, given some more time in developing its 'brain.' by this I mean that it will ultimately be able to actually make educated decisions based upon the facts given to it for true cognition, potentially including insight and intuition capabilities.  Watch out for this one.  It might be listening . . LOL
title: Beyond A.I. Potential

review:
It's fake. I'm not just saying this because I'm raging or I'm crazy or anything, I've actually put a lot of time into explaining how I know that it's fake. First of all, "cleverbot" consistently changes conversations, suggesting that there are multiple intelligences making up "cleverbot".  Although there are many theories, the one that I personally believe to be true is that instead of users talking to a Computer, they are simply talking to each other. This is how I think it works: a server automatically connects you with a different user, and sometimes will swap you to another one (to adjust for the ever changing numbers of users or the fact that a previous user has simply left. This would explains several things, first of all being that cleverbot frequently changes subjects would be the result of changing users. This would also explain why in the very description it states:" Cleverbot may attempt to connivence you that it is human" why else would they include this besides the obvious face that the so-called "Cleverbot" you are talking to actually IS a human. 

To anyone interested in downloading this app, i suggest that you don't. You would get the same (if not more) amount of entertainment by just texting someone.

review: This review is directed towards another review where the user felt nervous due to cleverbot asking him/her for their full name/parents name. Cleverbot is an AI (Artificial Intellegence) it is also a the of data collector. It learns more human dialogue everyday due to its ability to read,collect, and understand data that every person sends to it. So a user could be bored trying to figure out Cleverbot's fictional backstory and ask what it's parents names were hoping to get funny robotic names. In turn cleverbot collects this question and adds it to it's database of responses. The reason why it says to use with discretion is because simply... Cleverbot learns from the people he/she talks to. So if someone asks it something really dirty, it may ask you that same question later on because it learned it from someone else, just like us.
title: Very smart

review:
This app is horribly dangerous. You might as well go online and chat with people you don't know a thing about. Though the app claims it is artificial, it is clearly not: there are some obvious spelling mistakes; the dialogue is much too hard for a one dollar robot to understand and respond to; and finally, after I chatted with the "robot" for a while and summarized my point in this review, it replied: 'Darn! You found me out. My name is Kenny.'
I thought the robot's name was supposed to be Cleverbot..? The first thing I wondered when I looked at this app was: 'Why does this have to be connected to the Internet?'
Clearly, the reason is that people from around the world are sitting on their computer talking to whoever is using this app. I first began to feel discomfort when the "robot" starting asking me questions like where I live. I did not answer the question. I hope that if one of you iTunes customers dare to use this app after this review, you will not give out any personal information. Thank you for reading.

review:

Cleverbot is a learning bot, the data may not be stored, but he will learn more about communication as more people use him. His faulty spelling is proof. He's using information from his other users. And no, you are not switching through users. This is an actual bot. But, before buying the app, check out cleverbot . com , there you can try out Cleverbot for free and see if you enjoy. I enjoy using the app instead because I can keep up the conversation for however long. And remember, he isn't an bot for information. Don't go asking him for the weather. He's a bot for entertainment. 
Also, I really love Cleverbot!

review:
Hello,

Cleverbot is a computer website which was transformed into an iPhone application. Cleverbot takes what human beings type into the server, and records them, and uses them to communicate to the human who is using the application. It is a life-like robot which claims to be a human and says the actual human is a robot. The Review Guru® has tested this application on 10 different iPhones and these are the results: all 10 of the iPhones failed to perform. The robot cannot continue the conversation it is talking about. One minute you could be talking about computers and the next minute the robot could start talking about a banana. Also, in the settings you have the option to change the color of your conversation bubbles. On ten out of ten iPhones, this did not work. We gave Cleverbot three stars because the conversations do not continue. The Review Guru® recommends this games for ages ten and up due to some mild/infrequent inappropriate conversations. Good Luck.

Sincerely, 
 Review Guru® 
title:  Review Guru® 

review:
All who say it's switching users on you are just people who don't know anything about it so they make guesses and assumptions. 

The program had an is being developed all the time by people talking to it. It listens to what users say and stores aspects and uses it for other sessions, and it learns responses to questions by how you reply to them. It requires a connection with the server for this reason, because it retrieves the current program as well as uploading phrases you said to it.

This is the reason it calls you a bot, not because it's really human, but because users very often try to convince it that it's a bot. It has stored this and now says the same thing back to users. 

You can read about it on the cleverbot website. Instead of making claims out of ignorance, you should read on what this really is before downloading it perhaps?

title: SCAM

review:
For all the people who are saying that "it's not a bot, you're talking with a human" or "it doesn't understand what I  said", lighten up. It is, in fact, a computer program. A computer program is not perfect. Yes, you can mute the sounds and listen to your own music if you want. That's what settings are for, so you can set what you want it to be like.

The actual program is great. I sung Winter Wrap Up with it out of nowhere. It's really just something to do if you're bored and want to get angry. I say it's worth money, definitely.

That said, yes, there will be typos and mistakes, but that is not its fault for being random, it's other users who think that spitting out random stuff is okay, it's not, it ruins the program, henceforth making it more and more incorrect and unenjoyable for proceeding users

In the middle of that conversation, it said " can i call you snugglie pants?" get a sense of humor people! And besides, you can always get back to the same conversation. I love the app!
title: Love it!

review:
I'm giving this an honest, fair review. At times, I legitametly believed I was talking to another human, or forgot I was speaking with a bot. At times this app can be unbelievably intellegent, abd carry on actual, realistic and, at times, intellectual conversation.

 At the same time, this app amazes at how stupid it can be, changing subjects randomly, spazzing out, even crashing when it "does not compute". I understanf that it's just a bot, but these flaws can ruin the experence.

To all of you complaining at how the app pretends to be human, or says bizzarre things like ask for your bank account....that's the whole point. The app is supposed to be silly. You're supposed to wonder if it's really a bot or if it's really intellegent.

All in all this app can be frustrating, but I was so amazed at how intellegent it can be that I have to forgive it and give a star more than I would have otherwise.

title: A game worth something

review: Ok guys. Number one. It is not a real person. It just has a short memory and that's why it forgets what your talking about, not because it's a new person. Secondly, the reason it claims it's a person and swears because it takes info from different conversations it's had before. And lastly, the reason why the word bubbles change is because you're starting a new conversation. Only thing that could make it better is it having a longer memory.
title: Calm down! I'll fix your problems.

review: This is a bot. It's obviously hard to create a 100% accurate bot for the internet to appreciate... but if that DID happen, people would accuse it being another person talking to them. This is 100% a bot, and yes some responses are dumb. But it is entertaining nevertheless. What this app does is it uses other people's responses to make it more intelligent. For example, if cleverbot says hi, that means at some point that is what a user typed. Sort of like SimSimi. Judging on the average response to a comment, that's how often cleverbot will say it.
title: Guys, calm down.

Although it seems odd that the app makers would try to trick people like this it is still a good a for if you are bored or socially challenged like me......more on the subject as I discover.

For  $.99 it's DEFINITELY worth getting and trying out but I really hope the AI is improved.  It's predecessor SmarterChild was way more advanced and that was nearly a decade ago.

review:
Obviously Existor exists, they are a real company, but they really don't do anything with their products anymore. Rollo Carpenter seems to be more interested in making music and working on his books about the Metaverse, that's fine, but seeing Cleverbot, what was once so popular, essentially dead, is a little bit sad.

The app hasn't gotten any updates in 8 years at this point, and I'm well and truly surprised that it even functions anymore. It's missing quite a few features of the main Cleverbot website, such as being able to log in, snippets, so on and so forth, and it would be really nice to get a UI update at least, come on, not even dark mode?

What surprises me more though, is that Cleverbot is still running after all these years, it's still somewhat active, and even though it peaked around 2016/17 when a bunch of YouTubers started making videos on it, that's the exact year that the last blog post was ever posted on Existor's blog.

As for the app itself, it works fine, it allows you to keep your conversations, so that's pretty cool. I'm currently collecting a bit of Cleverbot's training data for my own chatbots, and who knows, maybe I'll make my own more modern version, app, or website for Cleverbot eventually.

- PF

review:
There seems to be a few things that need to be clarified once and for all:
1) Cleverbot is nothing but a program. The only human involved is yourself.
2) The reason that Cleverbot will sometimes insist that YOU are the robot is because Cleverbot learns from humans, who have obviously said, "You're a robot" to him a million times before. You are NEVER talking to another person. He only knows what other people tell him, so as far as he's concerned, he's the only sentient being on the planet.
All things considered, this is a some pretty sophisticated AI. Appreciate it for what it is. Besides, Cleverbot has won more awards and had more articles written about him that you have. So don't judge.

It generates it's AI by searching other people talking about the same thing.it takes those comments and matches it with something that makes sense. So when it says "I'm not a robot, you are" it's because REAL people, are stating they arn't a robot to cleverbot, you git matched with that. That is how this system works

title: What???????

review: When you realize what is really happening, that you are talking to other users and periodically switch who you are talking to, everything will make sense suddenly.  The responses are either people who know what's going on or people who don't know and get pissed off asking for proof.  Key words will change the conversation also.  I love it because it's so fun to mess with people.  Good for the creators for being so clever and I think it's worth the money.  ( seriously just 99 cents)
title: Love it

review: Those of you who think that you are taking to a human, you are wrong. Cleverbot is programed to learn from what other people say to it. The reason you need to be online is because the MASSIVE database of responses and code is too huge to be put on an app. And the reason it doesn't work to well is because people will corrupt it by "teaching" it wrong stuff by typing stupid random stuff. That is why I give to two stars. Because it has become too popular and is now corrupted.

title: Really?

review:
It's a robot. The app is NOT that elaborate, however. That's why you need an internet connection. Everything you say (and the context) is sent to the database on the internet.Originally, this was a website. Icogno wrote a simple iPhone client to connect you to cleverbot's database of statements. Its simpler than an IRC client! This doesn't even connect you to other people. This just scans the database and shows the most appropriate response. Now, on with the review.

This app is cheap and fun. If you enjoy silly chatting, this is the silliest you can get. I would honestly recommend this.
